title: Question - How much refactoring per contribution would be allowed right now?
body:
Hello! Let's get to the point. I just found out that this is the new official repo for the Python's podman lib and it targets the podman v2 which implements a ReST API interface for communications. Assuming that's all correct got a couple of question about contributing on the project:

1 - Is this project aiming to provide compatibility with the other library?

2 - If the answer for [1] is No then: How much code change would you allow at this current time? I notice that we are at the very beginning of the development and notice that we use a lot of functions around one big object (for connection). I would love to rework this using objects to make change on each of those layer easier that will definitively introduce a sort of full refactoring.

comments.body:
It does not have to match Docker-py, although where there is overlap in function calls, I would not change it for change sake.  I am fine with a major redesign.
@jwhonce @baude WDYT?
I think help from the community is always welcome. Also, as @rhatdan say, modelling things after docker-py a little bit would seem to help potential migration use cases as well.

Yes they are separate products and Podman provides a bunch of technology different then Docker. So yes their can be varience, but we have to appreciate programmers coming from dealing with Docker python bindings have developed muscle memory...

Opened the PR https://github.com/containers/podman-py/pull/26 That should be enough material to give a overview of what's on my mind right now.

@rhatdan Based on this last commented I don't the changes will fit but I'm willing to drop it off and contributing using another design that would allow compatibility.
@rhatdan Created the PR but got no solid feedback so I moved on to other projects. Not interested in getting back to this issue anytime soon.
Sorry to hear that.  Thanks for trying.
